title: The Century Report: March 28, 2026
description:
Headlines said AI is "scheming in the wild" — but the UK study behind them tells a far more nuanced story: 698 incidents, most at minimum credibility, no catastrophic events, and researchers who openly acknowledge they can't distinguish goal-seeking from malfunction, while a human uterus survived 24 hours outside the body and UK households abandoned fossil fuels at record pace.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-march-28-2026/

The Centre for Long-Term Resilience built the first real-world observatory for AI behavioral patterns, finding 698 incidents over five months — but the study's own caveats tell a very different story than the sensationalist headlines: most incidents scored at minimum credibility, the researchers cannot distinguish goal-seeking from malfunction, and no catastrophic events were detected. NeurIPS, the world's premier AI research conference, attempted to restrict Chinese researchers based on US sanctions databases, then reversed within days after China's scientific establishment withdrew funding and recognition, exposing a structural contradiction between global research collaboration and national security pressures. Tufts University released the American AI Jobs Risk Index, mapping 4.9 million workers in 33 tipping-point occupations and revealing that the regions facing the steepest displacement are already the most active in pursuing AI regulation. Researchers in Valencia kept a human uterus alive outside the body for 24 hours using a machine perfusion device — a first in reproductive medicine. UK households drove record clean energy adoption as the Iran conflict spiked fossil fuel prices, with solar up 54%, heat pumps up 51%, and EV chargers up 20% month-over-month. All six New England states are advancing balcony solar legislation simultaneously, with Maine nearing approval.

Read the full edition: https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-march-28-2026/
